top_data_source_actions.rationale: Starting pitcher quality, pitch traits, strikeout/walk profile and matchup context are high-leverage MLB signals.
Bullpen availability and workload affect late-game win probability, especially after starter exits.
Market no-vig probability and odds movement are essential baselines; model promotion should not occur without beating them.
Injury context is important but must be carefully timestamped to avoid stale or post-game leakage.
Confirmed lineup and top-order hitter context affect actual game strength and should be tracked before model promotion.

data_source_hints: xwOBA or wOBA-style top lineup aggregates
barrel rate / hard-hit rate / swing-miss context
top_data_source_actions.rationale: Statcast batting quality measures contact quality and batter skill signals that are closer to process than final score outcomes.
Park and weather context can affect run environment, totals, and matchup conditions.
Rest and travel can matter, but should be lower priority than pitcher, lineup, market and Statcast process signals.

recommendations: Prioritize P1 data-source gaps before adding model complexity.
Evaluate P1 ready features only in shadow models with walk-forward and calibration gates.
No tracking-only feature should enter active serving without evidence from OOS lift, baseline comparison and leakage review.
